questions.question: A metallic sphere of radius $6 \text{ cm}$ is melted and recast into the shape of a cylinder of radius $4 \text{ cm}$. Find the height of the cylinder.

steps: Equate the volume of the sphere to the volume of the cylinder.
Substitute the formula for the volume of a sphere $\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$ and cylinder $\pi r^2 h$.

questions.subtopic: Conversion of solid from one shape to another

questions.question: Two cubes each of volume $64 \text{ cm}^3$ are joined end to end. Find the surface area of the resulting cuboid.

steps: Find the edge length of the cube by taking the cube root of the volume.
Determine the dimensions of the new cuboid (length, breadth, height).
Apply the surface area formula $2(lb + bh + lh)$.
solution.derivation: a^3 = 64 \implies a = 4 \text{ cm}. \text{ New dimensions: } l=8, b=4, h=4. \text{ SA} = 2(8 \cdot 4 + 4 \cdot 4 + 8 \cdot 4) = 2(32 + 16 + 32) = 2(80) = 160 \text{ cm}^2

questions.question: A conical tent is $10 \text{ m}$ high and the radius of its base is $24 \text{ m}$. Find the slant height of the tent and the cost of the canvas required to make the tent, if the cost of $1 \text{ m}^2$ canvas is $\text{ ₹} 70$.

steps: Calculate the curved surface area (CSA) of the cone using $\pi rl$.
Multiply the CSA by the given cost per square meter.

questions.question: A medicine capsule is in the shape of a cylinder with two hemispheres stuck to each of its ends. The length of the entire capsule is $14 \text{ mm}$ and the diameter of the capsule is $5 \text{ mm}$. Find its surface area.

steps: Identify the cylindrical part length by subtracting the radii of the two hemispheres from the total length.
Calculate the CSA of the cylinder and the CSA of the two hemispheres.
Sum the two areas to find the total surface area.
solution.derivation: r = 2.5 \text{ mm}, h_{\text{cyl}} = 14 - (2.5 + 2.5) = 9 \text{ mm}. \text{ SA} = 2\pi rh + 2(2\pi r^2) = 2\pi r(h + 2r) = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 2.5 \times (9 + 5) = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 2.5 \times 14 = 220 \text{ mm}^2

questions.question: A hemispherical tank, full of water, is emptied by a pipe at the rate of $3\frac{4}{7}$ litres per second. How much time will it take to empty half the tank, if it is $3 \text{ m}$ in diameter? (Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)

steps: Calculate the volume of the hemispherical tank using $\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$.
Find half the volume and convert it to litres ($1 \text{ m}^3 = 1000 \text{ L}$).
Divide by the rate of flow and convert seconds to minutes.
solution.derivation: r = 1.5 \text{ m}. \text{ Vol} = \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times (1.5)^3 \approx 7.07 \text{ m}^3. \text{ Half Vol} = 3.535 \text{ m}^3 = 3535 \text{ L}. \text{ Rate} = \frac{25}{7} \text{ L/s}. \text{ Time} = 3535 \div \frac{25}{7} = 990 \text{ seconds} = 16.5 \text{ minutes}.
